final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Cognito User Management System with Admin-Only User Provisioning'

Parameters:
  DomainPrefix:
    Type: String
    Default: "myapp-login"
    AllowedPattern: "[a-z0-9-]+"
    Description: "Prefix for the Cognito domain (must be globally unique)"
  CallbackURLs:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Default: "https://localhost"
    Description: "List of allowed callback URLs for OAuth2.0"

Resources:
  UserPool:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPool
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      UserPoolName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-user-pool"
      AutoVerifiedAttributes:
        - email
      VerificationMessageTemplate:
        DefaultEmailOption: CONFIRM_WITH_CODE
        EmailMessage: "Your verification code is {####}"
        EmailSubject: "Verify your email for our application"
      Policies:
        PasswordPolicy:
          MinimumLength: 8
          RequireLowercase: true
          RequireNumbers: true
          RequireSymbols: false
          RequireUppercase: true
      AdminCreateUserConfig:
        AllowAdminCreateUserOnly: true
        UnusedAccountValidityDays: 7
      Schema:
        - Name: email
          AttributeDataType: String
          Required: true
          Mutable: true

  UserPoolClient:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPoolClient
    Properties:
      ClientName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-client"
      UserPoolId: !Ref UserPool
      GenerateSecret: true
      AllowedOAuthFlowsUserPoolClient: true
      AllowedOAuthFlows:
        - code
        - implicit
      AllowedOAuthScopes:
        - email
        - openid
        - profile
      CallbackURLs: !Ref CallbackURLs
      SupportedIdentityProviders:
        - COGNITO
      ExplicitAuthFlows:
        - ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH
        - ALLOW_REFRESH_TOKEN_AUTH
      ReadAttributes:
        - email
      WriteAttributes:
        - email

  UserPoolDomain:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPoolDomain
    Properties:
      Domain: !Ref DomainPrefix
      UserPoolId: !Ref UserPool

  ClientSecretSecret:
    Type: AWS::SecretsManager::Secret
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-cognito-client-secret"
      Description: "Stores the client secret for the Cognito user pool client"
      SecretString: !GetAtt UserPoolClient.ClientSecret

Outputs:
  UserPoolId:
    Description: "ID of the Cognito User Pool"
    Value: !Ref UserPool
  UserPoolClientId:
    Description: "ID of the Cognito User Pool Client"
    Value: !Ref UserPoolClient
  UserPoolDomainUrl:
    Description: "Cognito hosted login domain URL"
    Value: !Sub "https://${DomainPrefix}.auth.${AWS::Region}.amazoncognito.com"
  ClientSecretSecretArn:
    Description: "ARN of the Secrets Manager secret containing the client secret"
    Value: !Ref ClientSecretSecret

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Event-driven integration between EventBridge and a MongoDB API endpoint

Resources:
  MongoDBEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: my-mongodb-event-bus

  MongoDBConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: my-mongodb-connection
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: test_name
          ApiKeyValue: test_value

  MongoDBApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: my-mongodb-api-destination
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt MongoDBConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: https://www.testanu.com
      HttpMethod: POST
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 10

  DeadLetterQueueEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Customer-managed KMS key for DeadLetterQueue encryption
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root
            Action: kms:*
            Resource: '*'

  DeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueueEncryptionKey.Arn

  EventBridgeInvokeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgeInvokePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: events:InvokeApiDestination
                Resource: !GetAtt MongoDBApiDestination.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: sqs:SendMessage
                Resource: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kms:GenerateDataKey
                  - kms:Decrypt
                Resource: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueueEncryptionKey.Arn

  MongoDBEventRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      EventBusName: !Ref MongoDBEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - my.application
      Targets:
        - Id: MongoDBApiTarget
          Arn: !GetAtt MongoDBApiDestination.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeInvokeRole.Arn
          DeadLetterConfig:
            Arn: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn

Outputs:
  EventBusName:
    Description: Name of the event bus
    Value: !Ref MongoDBEventBus
  ApiDestinationArn:
    Description: ARN of the API destination
    Value: !GetAtt MongoDBApiDestination.Arn
  DeadLetterQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the dead-letter queue
    Value: !Ref DeadLetterQueue
  IamRoleArn:
    Description: ARN of the IAM role for EventBridge
    Value: !GetAtt EventBridgeInvokeRole.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Private API Gateway with Lambda backend and VPC endpoint'
Parameters:
  CustomDomainName:
    Type: String
    Default: api.example.com
    Description: Custom domain name for the API Gateway (e.g., api.example.com)
  VPCEndpointSecurityGroupName:
    Type: String
    Default: vpc-endpoint-sg
    Description: Name for the VPC endpoint security group
Resources:
  # VPC with DNS support and hostnames
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PrivateAPIVPC

  # Internet Gateway
  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PrivateAPIIGW

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  # Two public subnets across AZs
  Subnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicSubnet1

  Subnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.2.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicSubnet2

  # Public route table with default route to IGW
  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicRouteTable

  DefaultRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref Subnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref Subnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  # Security group for VPC endpoint
  VPCEndpointSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupName: !Ref VPCEndpointSecurityGroupName
      GroupDescription: Security group for execute-api VPC endpoint
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: vpc-endpoint-sg

  VPCEndpointSecurityGroupIngress:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress
    Properties:
      GroupId: !Ref VPCEndpointSecurityGroup
      IpProtocol: tcp
      FromPort: 443
      ToPort: 443
      CidrIp: !GetAtt VPC.CidrBlock

  # Interface VPC endpoint for execute-api
  VPCEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      ServiceName: !Sub com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.execute-api
      VpcEndpointType: Interface
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref Subnet1
        - !Ref Subnet2
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref VPCEndpointSecurityGroup
      PrivateDnsEnabled: true
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  # Lambda execution role
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

  # Lambda function with Python 3.12
  LambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Runtime: python3.12
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def handler(event, context):
              return {
                  'statusCode': 200,
                  'body': 'OK'
              }

  # Lambda permission for API Gateway with source ARN restriction
  LambdaPermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref LambdaFunction
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${RestApi}/*

  # IAM role for API Gateway to invoke Lambda
  ApiGatewayInvokeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: apigateway.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeLambdaPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt LambdaFunction.Arn

  # Private REST API
  RestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: PrivateApi
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - PRIVATE
        VpcEndpointIds:
          - !Ref VPCEndpoint
      Policy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: execute-api:Invoke
            Resource: '*'
            Condition:
              StringNotEquals:
                aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpoint
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action: execute-api:Invoke
            Resource: '*'

  # Proxy resource
  ProxyResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ParentId: !GetAtt RestApi.RootResourceId
      PathPart: '{proxy+}'

  # ANY method with Lambda integration
  ProxyMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref ProxyResource
      HttpMethod: ANY
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: AWS_PROXY
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${LambdaFunction.Arn}/invocations
        Credentials: !GetAtt ApiGatewayInvokeRole.Arn

  # Deployment
  ApiDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn: ProxyMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi

  # Stage
  ApiStage:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Stage
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      DeploymentId: !Ref ApiDeployment

  # Certificate for custom domain with EMAIL validation
  Certificate:
    Type: AWS::CertificateManager::Certificate
    Properties:
      DomainName: !Ref CustomDomainName
      ValidationMethod: EMAIL

  # Route53 Hosted Zone for the domain
  HostedZone:
    Type: AWS::Route53::HostedZone
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref CustomDomainName
      HostedZoneConfig:
        Comment: Hosted zone for custom domain

  # Custom domain name (V2) for private API Gateway
  ApiDomainName:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::DomainNameV2
    Properties:
      DomainName: !Ref CustomDomainName
      CertificateArn: !Ref Certificate
      EndpointAccessMode: PRIVATE
      SecurityPolicy: TLS_1_2

Outputs:
  ApiUrl:
    Description: Base URL of the private API via VPC endpoint
    Value: !Sub https://${ApiDomainName}
  VpcEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID
    Value: !Ref VPCEndpoint

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EventBridge integration with DataDog for centralized log forwarding

Parameters:
  DataDogApiKey:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    Default: testanu
    Description: DataDog API key used to authenticate log ingestion requests.

Resources:
  DeadLetterQueueKmsKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT
      KeySpec: SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: kms:*
            Resource: '*'
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sqs.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - kms:GenerateDataKey
              - kms:Decrypt
            Resource: '*'
            Condition:
              ArnLike:
                'kms:EncryptionContext:aws:sqs:queue-arn': !Sub 'arn:aws:sqs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:DeadLetterQueue-*'
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  DeadLetterQueueKmsKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub alias/DeadLetterQueue-${AWS::StackName}
      TargetKeyId: !Ref DeadLetterQueueKmsKey

  DeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub 'DeadLetterQueue-${AWS::StackName}'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref DeadLetterQueueKmsKey
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  DataDogSecret:
    Type: AWS::SecretsManager::Secret
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'DataDogApiKey-${AWS::StackName}'
      SecretString: !Ref DataDogApiKey
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  MyDataDogEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'MyDataDogEventBus-${AWS::StackName}'

  EventBridgeApiDestinationRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub 'EventBridgeApiDestinationRole-${AWS::StackName}'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeApiDestinationAndSendToDLQ
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: events:InvokeApiDestination
                Resource: !GetAtt DataDogLogIngestion.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: sqs:SendMessage
                Resource: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn

  DataDogConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'DataDogConnection-${AWS::StackName}'
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: DD-API-KEY
          ApiKeyValue: !Sub '{{resolve:secretsmanager:${DataDogSecret}}}'

  DataDogLogIngestion:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'DataDogLogIngestion-${AWS::StackName}'
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt DataDogConnection.Arn
      HttpMethod: POST
      InvocationEndpoint: 'https://http-intake.logs.datadoghq.com'
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 300

  DataDogLogForwardingRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'DataDogLogForwardingRule-${AWS::StackName}'
      EventBusName: !Ref MyDataDogEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - MyDataDogApp
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt DataDogLogIngestion.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeApiDestinationRole.Arn
          Id: DataDogLogTarget
          DeadLetterConfig:
            Arn: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn

Outputs:
  DeadLetterQueueUrl:
    Value: !Ref DeadLetterQueue
    Description: URL of the dead-letter SQS queue
  DataDogSecretArn:
    Value: !Ref DataDogSecret
    Description: ARN of the Secrets Manager secret storing the DataDog API key
  EventBusName:
    Value: !Ref MyDataDogEventBus
    Description: Custom EventBridge event bus name
  ApiDestinationArn:
    Value: !GetAtt DataDogLogIngestion.Arn
    Description: ARN of the EventBridge API destination forwarding logs to DataDog

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'AWS B2BI Pipeline for EDI X12_214 to JSON transformation'

Parameters:
  ProfileBusinessName:
    Type: String
    Default: 'MyBusiness'
    Description: 'Business name for the B2BI profile'
  ProfileName:
    Type: String
    Default: 'MyEDIProfile'
    Description: 'Name of the B2BI profile'
  ProfileEmail:
    Type: String
    Default: 'admin@example.com'
    Description: 'Contact email for B2BI profile'
  ProfilePhone:
    Type: String
    Default: '+1234567890'
    Description: 'Contact phone for B2BI profile'
  TransformerName:
    Type: String
    Default: 'X12_214_To_JSON_Transformer'
    Description: 'Name of the B2BI transformer'

Resources:
  BucketEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: "CMK for B2BI S3 bucket encryption"
      KeySpec: SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: Enable IAM User Permissions
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: kms:*
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: Allow S3 Service to Use Key
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: s3.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - kms:GenerateDataKey
              - kms:Decrypt
            Resource: '*'
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: B2BI-S3-EncryptionKey
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production

  BucketEncryptionKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: alias/b2bi-s3-encryption-key
      TargetKeyId: !Ref BucketEncryptionKey

  InputBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - BucketKeyEnabled: true
            ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              KMSMasterKeyID: !Ref BucketEncryptionKey
              SSEAlgorithm: aws:kms
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: B2BI-Input
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  OutputBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - BucketKeyEnabled: true
            ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              KMSMasterKeyID: !Ref BucketEncryptionKey
              SSEAlgorithm: aws:kms
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: B2BI-Output
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  B2BICapabilityRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: b2bi.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: B2BIBucketAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                Resource: !Sub '${InputBucket.Arn}/*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                Resource: !Sub '${OutputBucket.Arn}/*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kms:Decrypt
                  - kms:GenerateDataKey
                Resource: !GetAtt BucketEncryptionKey.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Role
          Value: B2BI-Capability

  Profile:
    Type: AWS::B2BI::Profile
    Properties:
      BusinessName: !Ref ProfileBusinessName
      Email: !Ref ProfileEmail
      Name: !Ref ProfileName
      Phone: !Ref ProfilePhone
      Logging: ENABLED
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Ref ProfileName
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production

  Transformer:
    Type: AWS::B2BI::Transformer
    Properties:
      EdiType:
        X12Details:
          TransactionSet: "X12_214"
      Mapping:
        TemplateLanguage: "JSONATA"
        Template: |
          {
            "mapping": [
              {"source": "X12_214/ST/transaction_set_identifier_code", "target": "transactionType"},
              {"source": "X12_214/ST/transaction_set_control_number", "target": "controlNumber"},
              {"source": "X12_214/BGN/date_time_reference/date", "target": "messageDate"},
              {"source": "X12_214/BGN/date_time_reference/time", "target": "messageTime"},
              {"source": "X12_214/N1/entity_identifier_code", "target": "senderId"},
              {"source": "X12_214/N1/name", "target": "senderName"},
              {"source": "X12_214/LX/assigned_number", "target": "segmentNumber"},
              {"source": "X12_214/Q7/status_reason_code", "target": "statusReason"},
              {"source": "X12_214/SE/number_of_included_segments", "target": "totalSegments"}
            ]
          }
      Name: !Ref TransformerName
      Status: active
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Ref TransformerName
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production

  Capability:
    Type: AWS::B2BI::Capability
    Properties:
      Configuration:
        Edi:
          InputLocation:
            BucketName: !Ref InputBucket
            Key: ""
          OutputLocation:
            BucketName: !Ref OutputBucket
            Key: ""
          TransformerId: !Ref Transformer
      Type: edi
      InstructionsDocuments: []
      Name: !Sub '${TransformerName}-InboundCapability'
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${TransformerName}-InboundCapability'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production

Outputs:
  InputBucketName:
    Description: "Name of the input S3 bucket for raw EDI files"
    Value: !Ref InputBucket
  OutputBucketName:
    Description: "Name of the output S3 bucket for transformed JSON"
    Value: !Ref OutputBucket
  ProfileId:
    Description: "B2BI Profile ID"
    Value: !Ref Profile
  TransformerId:
    Description: "B2BI Transformer ID"
    Value: !Ref Transformer
  CapabilityId:
    Description: "B2BI Capability ID"
    Value: !Ref Capability
  CapabilityRoleArn:
    Description: "IAM Role ARN used by B2BI capability"
    Value: !GetAtt B2BICapabilityRole.Arn
  EncryptionKeyArn:
    Description: "ARN of the KMS CMK used for S3 bucket encryption"
    Value: !GetAtt BucketEncryptionKey.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "Automated monitoring and alerting for AWS Secrets Manager lifecycle events"
Parameters:
  EmailAddress:
    Type: String
    AllowedPattern: "^[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\\.[A-Za-z]{2,}$"
    Description: "Email address to receive Secrets Manager notifications"
    Default: "user@example.com"
Resources:
  SecretsManagerKmsKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: "KMS key for encrypting Secrets Manager SNS topic"
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: "Enable IAM User Permissions"
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: "kms:*"
            Resource: "*"
          - Sid: "Allow SNS Service to use the key"
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sns.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - "kms:GenerateDataKey*"
              - "kms:Decrypt"
            Resource: "*"
      KeySpec: SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT
      PendingWindowInDays: 7
  SecretsManagerNotificationTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      DisplayName: "SecretsManager-Alerts"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SecretsManagerKmsKey
  EmailSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      TopicArn: !Ref SecretsManagerNotificationTopic
      Protocol: email
      Endpoint: !Ref EmailAddress
  SecretsManagerTopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref SecretsManagerNotificationTopic
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sns:Publish
            Resource: !Ref SecretsManagerNotificationTopic
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !GetAtt SecretsManagerEventRule.Arn
  SecretsManagerEventRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Description: "Captures Secrets Manager lifecycle events and publishes to SNS"
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - aws.secretsmanager
        detail-type:
          - "Secret Version Stage Change"
          - "Secret Version Deletion"
          - "Secret Rotation Success"
          - "Secret Rotation Failure"
      Targets:
        - Arn: !Ref SecretsManagerNotificationTopic
          Id: "SecretsManagerSNSTarget"
Outputs:
  SnsTopicArn:
    Description: "ARN of the SNS topic for Secrets Manager alerts"
    Value: !Ref SecretsManagerNotificationTopic
  EventBridgeRuleName:

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "SNS-to-SQS fanout system with filtered metric message routing"

Resources:
  # Customer managed KMS key for encrypting SNS topic and SQS queues
  MetricsEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      Description: "Customer managed key for SNS and SQS encryption"
      Enabled: true
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT
      PendingWindowInDays: 7
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: "Enable root account full access"
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: "kms:*"
            Resource: "*"
          - Sid: "Allow SNS service to use the key"
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sns.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - kms:GenerateDataKey*
              - kms:Decrypt
            Resource: "*"
          - Sid: "Allow SQS service to use the key"
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sqs.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - kms:GenerateDataKey*
              - kms:Decrypt
            Resource: "*"

  # Central SNS Topic for all metric messages
  CentralSnsTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: "CentralMetricsTopic"
      DisplayName: "Central Metrics Topic"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey

  # Dead-letter queues for each main queue
  AllMetricsDlq:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: "AllMetricsDlq"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600  # 4 days
      VisibilityTimeout: 30

  TemperatureDlq:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: "TemperatureDlq"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30

  HumidityDlq:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: "HumidityDlq"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30

  # Main queues with dead-letter configuration
  AllMetricsSqsQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: "AllMetricsQueue"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600  # 4 days
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt AllMetricsDlq.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5

  TemperatureSqsQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: "TemperatureQueue"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt TemperatureDlq.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5

  HumiditySqsQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: "HumidityQueue"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref MetricsEncryptionKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt HumidityDlq.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5

  # SNS Subscriptions with filter policies
  AllMetricsSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      TopicArn: !Ref CentralSnsTopic
      Endpoint: !GetAtt AllMetricsSqsQueue.Arn
      Protocol: sqs
      RawMessageDelivery: true

  TemperatureSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      TopicArn: !Ref CentralSnsTopic
      Endpoint: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueue.Arn
      Protocol: sqs
      RawMessageDelivery: true
      FilterPolicy:
        MetricType:
          - "Temperature"

  HumiditySubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      TopicArn: !Ref CentralSnsTopic
      Endpoint: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueue.Arn
      Protocol: sqs
      RawMessageDelivery: true
      FilterPolicy:
        MetricType:
          - "Humidity"

  # Queue Policies granting SNS permission to send messages to each queue
  AllMetricsQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref AllMetricsSqsQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sns.amazonaws.com
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt AllMetricsSqsQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref CentralSnsTopic

  TemperatureQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref TemperatureSqsQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sns.amazonaws.com
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref CentralSnsTopic

  HumidityQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref HumiditySqsQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sns.amazonaws.com
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref CentralSnsTopic

Outputs:
  CentralSnsTopicArn:
    Description: "ARN of the central SNS topic"
    Value: !Ref CentralSnsTopic
  AllMetricsQueueArn:
    Description: "ARN of the All-Metrics SQS queue"
    Value: !GetAtt AllMetricsSqsQueue.Arn
  TemperatureQueueArn:
    Description: "ARN of the Temperature SQS queue"
    Value: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueue.Arn
  HumidityQueueArn:
    Description: "ARN of the Humidity SQS queue"
    Value: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueue.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Event-driven integration between DynamoDB streams and EventBridge using Pipes to route INSERT and MODIFY events to a centralized event bus.

Parameters:
  TableName:
    Type: String
    Default: OrdersTable
    Description: Name of the DynamoDB table.
  EventBusName:
    Type: String
    Default: OrderEventBus
    Description: Name of the EventBridge event bus.

Resources:
  OrdersTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      TableName: !Ref TableName
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: OrderId
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: OrderId
          KeyType: HASH
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      StreamSpecification:
        StreamViewType: NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES

  OrderEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref EventBusName

  PipeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: pipes.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: PipePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - dynamodb:DescribeStream
                  - dynamodb:GetRecords
                  - dynamodb:GetShardIterator
                  - dynamodb:ListStreams
                Resource: !GetAtt OrdersTable.StreamArn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:PutEvents
                Resource: !GetAtt OrderEventBus.Arn

  InsertPipe:
    Type: AWS::Pipes::Pipe
    Properties:
      Source: !GetAtt OrdersTable.StreamArn
      Target: !GetAtt OrderEventBus.Arn
      RoleArn: !GetAtt PipeRole.Arn
      SourceParameters:
        DynamoDBStreamParameters:
          BatchSize: 1
          MaximumBatchingWindowInSeconds: 0
          StartingPosition: LATEST
        FilterCriteria:
          Filters:
            - Pattern: '{"eventName": ["INSERT"]}'

  ModifyPipe:
    Type: AWS::Pipes::Pipe
    Properties:
      Source: !GetAtt OrdersTable.StreamArn
      Target: !GetAtt OrderEventBus.Arn
      RoleArn: !GetAtt PipeRole.Arn
      SourceParameters:
        DynamoDBStreamParameters:
          BatchSize: 1
          MaximumBatchingWindowInSeconds: 0
          StartingPosition: LATEST
        FilterCriteria:
          Filters:
            - Pattern: '{"eventName": ["MODIFY"]}'

Outputs:
  TableName:
    Description: The DynamoDB table name.
    Value: !Ref OrdersTable
  TableStreamArn:
    Description: The DynamoDB table stream ARN.
    Value: !GetAtt OrdersTable.StreamArn
  EventBusArn:
    Description: The EventBridge event bus ARN.
    Value: !GetAtt OrderEventBus.Arn
  InsertPipeArn:
    Description: The ARN of the INSERT pipe.
    Value: !GetAtt InsertPipe.Arn
  ModifyPipeArn:
    Description: The ARN of the MODIFY pipe.
    Value: !GetAtt ModifyPipe.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'EventBridge Pipes workflow to enrich SQS messages with Zippopotam API data and log results'

Resources:
  # Customer-managed KMS key for SQS queue encryption
  SQSQueueKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Customer-managed KMS key for SQS queue encryption
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: kms:*
            Resource: "*"
      PendingWindowInDays: 7
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT

  # SQS Queue as event source
  SourceQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: EventPipeSourceQueue
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SQSQueueKey

  # CloudWatch Log Group as target (kept for reference, though Pipe uses Lambda target)
  LogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: EventPipeLogGroup
      RetentionInDays: 7

  # IAM Role for Lambda functions
  LambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaLogPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: !Sub "arn:${AWS::Partition}:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:*:*"

  # Lambda function that enriches messages by calling Zippopotam API
  EnrichmentLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: ZippopotamEnrichmentLambda
      Runtime: python3.12
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 30
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import urllib.request
          import urllib.error

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              # Extract the zip code from the event
              try:
                  # SQS Pipe source sends the message body directly
                  if isinstance(event, str):
                      body = event
                  elif isinstance(event, dict):
                      body = event.get('body', json.dumps(event))
                  else:
                      body = str(event)

                  # Try to parse body as JSON, extract zip field or use raw
                  try:
                      body_obj = json.loads(body)
                      if isinstance(body_obj, dict):
                          zip_code = body_obj.get('zip', body_obj.get('Zip', body.strip()))
                      else:
                          zip_code = str(body_obj).strip()
                  except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
                      zip_code = body.strip()
              except Exception:
                  zip_code = ''

              if not zip_code:
                  return {"error": "Could not extract zip code from event", "event": event}

              # Call the Zippopotam API
              url = f"https://api.zippopotam.us/us/{zip_code}"
              try:
                  req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'EventBridgePipe/1.0'})
                  with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20) as response:
                      data = response.read()
                      result = json.loads(data.decode('utf-8'))
                      return result
              except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
                  return {"error": f"HTTP {e.code}: {e.reason}", "zip": zip_code}
              except urllib.error.URLError as e:
                  return {"error": f"URL error: {e.reason}", "zip": zip_code}
              except Exception as e:
                  return {"error": str(e), "zip": zip_code}

  # Lambda function that logs enriched data to CloudWatch
  LogTargetLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: EnrichedDataLogger
      Runtime: python3.12
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 10
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              print("ENRICHED EVENT:")
              print(json.dumps(event, indent=2))
              return {"statusCode": 200, "body": json.dumps({"logged": True})}

  # Permission for EventBridge Pipes to invoke the enrichment Lambda
  EnrichmentLambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref EnrichmentLambda
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: pipes.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt EventPipe.Arn

  # Permission for EventBridge Pipes to invoke the target Lambda
  LogTargetLambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref LogTargetLambda
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: pipes.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt EventPipe.Arn

  # IAM Role for EventBridge Pipes
  PipeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: pipes.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: PipePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sqs:ReceiveMessage
                  - sqs:DeleteMessage
                  - sqs:GetQueueAttributes
                Resource: !GetAtt SourceQueue.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt EnrichmentLambda.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt LogTargetLambda.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: !GetAtt LogGroup.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kms:Decrypt
                  - kms:GenerateDataKey
                Resource: !GetAtt SQSQueueKey.Arn

  # EventBridge Pipe
  EventPipe:
    Type: AWS::Pipes::Pipe
    Properties:
      Name: EventPipe
      RoleArn: !GetAtt PipeRole.Arn
      Source: !GetAtt SourceQueue.Arn
      SourceParameters:
        SqsQueueParameters:
          BatchSize: 1
      Enrichment: !GetAtt EnrichmentLambda.Arn
      Target: !GetAtt LogTargetLambda.Arn

Outputs:
  SourceQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the SQS source queue
    Value: !Ref SourceQueue
  PipeArn:
    Description: ARN of the EventBridge Pipe
    Value: !GetAtt EventPipe.Arn
  LogGroupName:
    Description: Name of the CloudWatch Log Group
    Value: !Ref LogGroup
  EnrichmentLambdaArn:
    Description: ARN of the enrichment Lambda function
    Value: !GetAtt EnrichmentLambda.Arn
  LogTargetLambdaArn:
    Description: ARN of the logging target Lambda function
    Value: !GetAtt LogTargetLambda.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "EventBridge-to-Kinesis integration for real-time event streaming from a custom application."
Parameters:
  ApplicationSource:
    Type: String
    Default: "my-application"
    Description: "The source value used in events to match the rule."
Resources:
  AppEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "AppEventBus-${AWS::StackName}"
      Description: "Custom event bus for application-generated events."

  KinesisStreamKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: "kms:*"
            Resource: "*"
      Description: "KMS key for encrypting Kinesis stream data"
      Enabled: true

  KinesisStreamKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: "alias/AppEventStreamKey"
      TargetKeyId: !Ref KinesisStreamKey

  KinesisStream:
    Type: AWS::Kinesis::Stream
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      ShardCount: 1
      RetentionPeriodHours: 24
      Name: !Sub "AppEventStream-${AWS::StackName}"
      StreamEncryption:
        EncryptionType: KMS
        KeyId: !Ref KinesisStreamKeyAlias

  EventBridgeToKinesisRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgeToKinesisPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kinesis:PutRecord
                  - kinesis:PutRecords
                Resource: !GetAtt KinesisStream.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kms:GenerateDataKey
                Resource: !GetAtt KinesisStreamKey.Arn

  AppToKinesisRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      EventBusName: !Ref AppEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - !Ref ApplicationSource
      State: ENABLED
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt KinesisStream.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeToKinesisRole.Arn
          Id: "KinesisTarget"
          KinesisParameters:
            PartitionKeyPath: "$.detail-type"

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Event-driven AWS Batch processing pipeline with SQS, Lambda, and EventBridge Pipes

Parameters:
  VpcCIDR:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/16
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC
  PrivateSubnet1CIDR:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.1.0/24
    Description: CIDR block for the first private subnet
  PrivateSubnet2CIDR:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.2.0/24
    Description: CIDR block for the second private subnet

Resources:
  # Customer managed KMS key for SQS encryption
  QueueEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: KMS key for SQS queue encryption
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'

  QueueEncryptionKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: alias/QueueEncryptionKey
      TargetKeyId: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey

  # VPC
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCIDR
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-VPC'

  # Private Route Table
  PrivateRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-PrivateRT'

  # Private subnets in two AZs
  PrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      CidrBlock: !Ref PrivateSubnet1CIDR
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-PrivateSubnet1'

  PrivateSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      CidrBlock: !Ref PrivateSubnet2CIDR
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-PrivateSubnet2'

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  # Security group for Batch compute environment
  BatchSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for Batch compute instances
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: !Ref VpcCIDR
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-BatchSG'

  # VPC Endpoints
  S3GatewayEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      ServiceName: !Sub 'com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.s3'
      VpcEndpointType: Gateway
      RouteTableIds:
        - !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  ECRApiEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      ServiceName: !Sub 'com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.ecr.api'
      VpcEndpointType: Interface
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PrivateSubnet1
        - !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref BatchSecurityGroup
      PrivateDnsEnabled: true

  ECRDockerEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      ServiceName: !Sub 'com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.ecr.dkr'
      VpcEndpointType: Interface
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PrivateSubnet1
        - !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref BatchSecurityGroup
      PrivateDnsEnabled: true

  # SQS Dead Letter Queue
  DeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-DLQ'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600  # 14 days
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey

  # Main SQS Queue
  MainQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-EventQueue'
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey

  # IAM Roles
  BatchServiceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: batch.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSBatchServiceRole

  EC2InstanceProfileRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonEC2ContainerServiceforEC2Role
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: ECRPull
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
                  - ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability
                  - ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer
                  - ecr:BatchGetImage
                Resource: '*'

  ECSTaskExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: ECRPull
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
                  - ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability
                  - ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer
                  - ecr:BatchGetImage
                Resource: '*'

  PipeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: pipes.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: PipePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sqs:ReceiveMessage
                  - sqs:DeleteMessage
                  - sqs:GetQueueAttributes
                Resource: !GetAtt MainQueue.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt BatchJobSubmitter.Arn

  LambdaBatchRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaBatchPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - batch:SubmitJob
                Resource: '*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - iam:PassRole
                Resource: !GetAtt ECSTaskExecutionRole.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: '*'

  # EC2 Instance Profile for Batch compute
  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceProfileRole

  # Batch Compute Environment
  ComputeEnvironment:
    Type: AWS::Batch::ComputeEnvironment
    Properties:
      Type: MANAGED
      ServiceRole: !GetAtt BatchServiceRole.Arn
      ComputeResources:
        Type: EC2
        AllocationStrategy: BEST_FIT_PROGRESSIVE
        MinvCpus: 0
        DesiredvCpus: 0
        MaxvCpus: 256
        InstanceTypes:
          - optimal
        Subnets:
          - !Ref PrivateSubnet1
          - !Ref PrivateSubnet2
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref BatchSecurityGroup
        InstanceRole: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      State: ENABLED

  # Batch Job Queue
  JobQueue:
    Type: AWS::Batch::JobQueue
    Properties:
      Priority: 1
      ComputeEnvironmentOrder:
        - Order: 1
          ComputeEnvironment: !Ref ComputeEnvironment
      State: ENABLED

  # Batch Job Definition
  JobDefinition:
    Type: AWS::Batch::JobDefinition
    Properties:
      Type: container
      ContainerProperties:
        Image: public.ecr.aws/lts/ubuntu:latest
        Command:
          - echo
          - "Job triggered for OrderCreated"
        ExecutionRoleArn: !GetAtt ECSTaskExecutionRole.Arn
        ResourceRequirements:
          - Type: VCPU
            Value: '1'
          - Type: MEMORY
            Value: '512'
        Environment:
          - Name: MESSAGE
            Value: "OrderCreated"
      RetryStrategy:
        Attempts: 1
      Timeout:
        AttemptDurationSeconds: 300

  # Lambda function to submit Batch jobs
  BatchJobSubmitter:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Runtime: python3.12
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaBatchRole.Arn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          JOB_DEFINITION_ARN: !Ref JobDefinition
          JOB_QUEUE_ARN: !Ref JobQueue
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import boto3
          import os

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              batch_client = boto3.client('batch')
              job_definition = os.environ['JOB_DEFINITION_ARN']
              job_queue = os.environ['JOB_QUEUE_ARN']

              # Process each record from the Pipe/SQS message
              for record in event:
                  body = record.get('body', '')
                  response = batch_client.submit_job(
                      jobName='PipeTriggeredJob',
                      jobQueue=job_queue,
                      jobDefinition=job_definition,
                      containerOverrides={
                          'environment': [
                              {'name': 'MESSAGE', 'value': body}
                          ]
                      }
                  )
                  print(f"Submitted job {response['jobId']} for message: {body}")

              return {'statusCode': 200, 'body': 'Jobs submitted'}

  # EventBridge Pipe
  Pipe:
    Type: AWS::Pipes::Pipe
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-Pipe'
      RoleArn: !GetAtt PipeRole.Arn
      Source: !GetAtt MainQueue.Arn
      SourceParameters:
        SqsQueueParameters:
          BatchSize: 1
        FilterCriteria:
          Filters:
            - Pattern: '{"body": [{"prefix": "OrderCreated"}]}'
      Target: !GetAtt BatchJobSubmitter.Arn

Outputs:
  VpcId:
    Value: !Ref VPC
  PrivateSubnetIds:
    Value: !Join [',', [!Ref PrivateSubnet1, !Ref PrivateSubnet2]]
  SecurityGroupId:
    Value: !Ref BatchSecurityGroup
  ComputeEnvironmentArn:
    Value: !Ref ComputeEnvironment
  JobQueueArn:
    Value: !Ref JobQueue
  JobDefinitionArn:
    Value: !Ref JobDefinition
  MainQueueUrl:
    Value: !Ref MainQueue
  DeadLetterQueueUrl:
    Value: !Ref DeadLetterQueue
  LambdaFunctionArn:
    Value: !GetAtt BatchJobSubmitter.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "Scheduled cross-platform SSM automation via EventBridge Scheduler"
Metadata:
  cfn-lint:
    config:
      ignore_checks:
        - W1030
        - W2506

Parameters:
  VpcCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: "10.0.0.0/16"
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t2.micro
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
    Description: EC2 instance type for both instances
  LinuxAmi:
    Type: String
    Default: resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2
    Description: AMI ID for Linux instance resolved via SSM
  WindowsAmi:
    Type: String
    Default: resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-windows-latest/Windows_Server-2022-English-Full-Base
    Description: AMI ID for Windows instance resolved via SSM

Resources:
  # VPC
  Vpc:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCidr
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-vpc"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-igw"

  VpcGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      CidrBlock: "10.0.1.0/24"
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs ""]
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-public-subnet-1"

  PublicSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      CidrBlock: "10.0.2.0/24"
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs ""]
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-public-subnet-2"

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-public-rt"

  DefaultPublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VpcGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  # Security Groups
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for SSM managed instances
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: !Ref VpcCidr
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-sg-instance"

  VpcEndpointSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for VPC endpoints
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: !Ref VpcCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: !Ref VpcCidr
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-sg-vpc-endpoint"

  # VPC Endpoints
  S3GatewayEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      ServiceName: !Sub "com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.s3"
      VpcEndpointType: Gateway
      RouteTableIds:
        - !Ref PublicRouteTable

  SsmInterfaceEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      ServiceName: !Sub "com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.ssm"
      VpcEndpointType: Interface
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref VpcEndpointSecurityGroup

  SsmMessagesInterfaceEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      ServiceName: !Sub "com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.ssmmessages"
      VpcEndpointType: Interface
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref VpcEndpointSecurityGroup

  Ec2MessagesInterfaceEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref Vpc
      ServiceName: !Sub "com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.ec2messages"
      VpcEndpointType: Interface
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref VpcEndpointSecurityGroup

  # Launch Template for IMDSv2 enforcement
  InstanceLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateData:
        MetadataOptions:
          HttpTokens: required
          HttpEndpoint: enabled

  # IAM Roles and Instance Profile
  EC2Role:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Path: "/"

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2Role

  # EC2 Instances
  LinuxInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref LinuxAmi
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref InstanceLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt InstanceLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            Encrypted: true
      Tags:
        - Key: HelloWorld
          Value: "true"
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-linux"

  WindowsInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref WindowsAmi
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref InstanceLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt InstanceLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/sda1
          Ebs:
            Encrypted: true
      Tags:
        - Key: HelloWorld
          Value: "true"
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-windows"

  # SSM Document
  SsmDocument:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Document
    Properties:
      DocumentType: Command
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-HelloWorldDocument"
      Content:
        schemaVersion: "2.2"
        description: "Cross-platform hello world script"
        mainSteps:
          - name: linuxCommand
            action: aws:runShellScript
            precondition:
              StringEquals:
                - platformType
                - Linux
            inputs:
              runCommand:
                - 'echo "Hello World!"'
          - name: windowsCommand
            action: aws:runPowerShellScript
            precondition:
              StringEquals:
                - platformType
                - Windows
            inputs:
              runCommand:
                - 'Write-Output "Hello World!"'

  # SSM Association (no schedule – triggered by EventBridge)
  SsmAssociation:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Association
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref SsmDocument
      Targets:
        - Key: "tag:HelloWorld"
          Values:
            - "true"
      OutputLocation:
        S3Location:
          OutputS3BucketName: !Ref LogBucket
          OutputS3KeyPrefix: "ssm-logs/"
      AssociationName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-hello-world-association"

  # KMS Key for S3 encryption
  KmsKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: Allow root user admin
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: "kms:*"
            Resource: "*"
          - Sid: Allow SSM service
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ssm.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - kms:GenerateDataKey
              - kms:Decrypt
              - kms:DescribeKey
            Resource: "*"
      KeySpec: SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT
      KeyUsage: ENCRYPT_DECRYPT
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-kms-s3"

  KmsAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub "alias/${AWS::StackName}-s3-key"
      TargetKeyId: !Ref KmsKey

  # S3 Bucket for logs
  LogBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: aws:kms
              KMSMasterKeyID: !Ref KmsKey
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-log-bucket"

  LogBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref LogBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowSSMWrite
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ssm.amazonaws.com
            Action: s3:PutObject
            Resource: !Sub "${LogBucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption-aws-kms-key-id: !Ref KmsKey

  # IAM Role for EventBridge Scheduler
  EventBridgeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: scheduler.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SSMStartAssociation
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: ssm:StartAssociationsOnce
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:association/${SsmAssociation}"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:ListBucket
                  - s3:GetBucketLocation
                Resource: !GetAtt LogBucket.Arn

  # EventBridge Scheduler Schedule
  SchedulerSchedule:
    Type: AWS::Scheduler::Schedule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-hello-world-schedule"
      FlexibleTimeWindow:
        Mode: FLEXIBLE
        MaximumWindowInMinutes: 15
      ScheduleExpression: "rate(15 minutes)"
      ScheduleExpressionTimezone: UTC
      Target:
        Arn: !Sub "arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:association/${SsmAssociation}"
        RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeRole.Arn
        Input: "{}"

Outputs:
  LinuxInstanceId:
    Description: ID of the Linux EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref LinuxInstance
  WindowsInstanceId:
    Description: ID of the Windows EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref WindowsInstance
  LogBucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket storing SSM execution logs
    Value: !Ref LogBucket
  ScheduleArn:
    Description: ARN of the EventBridge Scheduler schedule
    Value: !GetAtt SchedulerSchedule.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Parameters:
  ShopName:
    Type: String
    Default: testanu
    Description: Name of the Shopify shop (e.g., testanu)
  ApiVersion:
    Type: String
    Default: "2024-01"
    Description: Shopify admin API version
  ShopifyApiKey:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    Default: "placeholder-api-key"
    Description: Shopify admin API key (X-Shopify-Access-Token)

Resources:
  # Event bus to capture events from MyShopifyApp
  ShopifyEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "shopify-events-${ShopName}"

  # Connection with API key authorization
  ShopifyConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: "X-Shopify-Access-Token"
          ApiKeyValue: !Ref ShopifyApiKey
      Name: !Sub "shopify-connection-${ShopName}"
      Description: Connection to Shopify admin API

  # API destination for product creation endpoint
  ShopifyApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt ShopifyConnection.Arn
      HttpMethod: POST
      InvocationEndpoint: !Sub "https://${ShopName}.myshopify.com/admin/api/${ApiVersion}/products.json"
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 10
      Name: !Sub "shopify-create-product-${ShopName}"
      Description: API destination for creating Shopify products

  # IAM role for EventBridge to invoke the API destination
  ShopifyEventRuleRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeShopifyApiDestination
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: events:InvokeApiDestination
                Resource: !GetAtt ShopifyApiDestination.Arn

  # Event rule that matches MyShopifyApp events and sends to API destination
  ShopifyProductRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      EventBusName: !Ref ShopifyEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "MyShopifyApp"
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt ShopifyApiDestination.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt ShopifyEventRuleRole.Arn
          Id: "ShopifyCreateProduct"
          InputTransformer:
            InputPathsMap:
              title: "$.detail.title"
              description: "$.detail.description"
              price: "$.detail.price"
            InputTemplate: |
              {
                "product": {
                  "title": <title>,
                  "body_html": <description>,
                  "variants": [
                    {
                      "price": <price>
                    }
                  ]
                }
              }

Outputs:
  EventBusArn:
    Description: ARN of the custom EventBridge event bus
    Value: !GetAtt ShopifyEventBus.Arn
  ApiDestinationArn:
    Description: ARN of the API destination for Shopify product creation
    Value: !GetAtt ShopifyApiDestination.Arn
